title: Clerodendrum ovalifolium
taxonomy: Clerodendrum ovalifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant is also known as the 'Tree Clerodendrum' or 'Chinese Glorybower'. Clerodendrum ovalifolium is classified under the genus Clerodendrum and is a member of the Verbenaceae family. The plant is further classified as belonging to the order Lamiales.
uses: Clerodendrum ovalifolium is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. In some cultures, the plant's leaves are applied topically to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. The plant is also believed to have antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating skin infections and wounds. Furthermore, Clerodendrum ovalifolium is used to treat fever and other respiratory issues. Additionally, it is sometimes used as a natural remedy for digestive problems.

intro: Clerodendrum ovalifolium, also known as the Butterfly Bush or Glory Bower, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and fragrant, showy flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Clerodendrum ovalifolium include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Clerodendrum ovalifolium is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of powder or extract to treat various health conditions.
